```json
{
  "name": "Add ONNX Backend",
  "overview": "Introduce a second inference backend for &#96;.onnx&#96; models while preserving the existing DeepStack-shaped REST contract. Keep the REST layer backend-agnostic, reuse Ultralytics/ONNX Runtime where possible to minimize custom code, and publish a new &#96;-onnx&#96; container artifact with CPU/GPU selection.",
  "plan": "# ONNX Backend Integration Plan\n\n## Fixed Decisions\n- GPU means &#96;NVIDIA CUDA&#96; in Docker.\n- Publish a new image artifact ending in &#96;-onnx&#96;.\n- Favor &#96;minimum bespoke code&#96; and &#96;broad ONNX compatibility&#96;, so the ONNX path should be &#96;Ultralytics-first&#96;, with ONNX Runtime underneath instead of hand-maintaining multiple output decoders unless forced.\n\n## Current State And Why It Must Change\nThe repo is small, but the current architecture hard-couples the REST server to the TFLite implementation:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;64:66:main.py\nlogger.info(\"Initializing YOLO detector...\")\ndetector = YOLOFLite(model_file, labels, confidence_threshold, intersection_over_union_threshold, device)\nlogger.info(\"YOLO detector initialized successfully.\")\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nRelevant surfaces:\n- [&#96;main.py&#96;](main.py): owns CLI parsing, backend construction, FastAPI routes, and server boot in one file.\n- [&#96;YOLOFLite.py&#96;](YOLOFLite.py): TFLite/EdgeTPU-specific preprocessing, inference, and postprocessing.\n- [&#96;prediction.py&#96;](prediction.py): the REST response contract that must remain stable.\n- [&#96;Dockerfile&#96;](Dockerfile): EdgeTPU-specific image; unsuitable as the basis for an ONNX image.\n- [&#96;requirements.txt&#96;](requirements.txt): TFLite-only runtime dependency set.\n- [&#96;.github/workflows/publish.yml&#96;](.github/workflows/publish.yml): currently publishes only one image.\n\n## External Constraints From Upstream Docs\n- Ultralytics ONNX export supports &#96;dynamic&#96;, &#96;simplify&#96;, &#96;opset&#96;, and optional &#96;nms&#96;; output structure is not guaranteed to match the current TFLite-specific postprocessor for all exports. Source: [Ultralytics export docs](https://docs.ultralytics.com/modes/export/) and [Ultralytics ONNX docs](https://docs.ultralytics.com/integrations/onnx/).\n- ONNX Runtime GPU packaging is provider-specific, not just a runtime flag. &#96;onnxruntime-gpu&#96; implies CUDA/cuDNN compatibility constraints, and provider ordering is explicit via &#96;InferenceSession(..., providers=[...])&#96;. Source: [ORT install docs](https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/install/), [CUDA EP docs](https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/execution-providers/CUDA-ExecutionProvider.html), and [Python API summary](https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/api/python/api_summary.html).\n- Docker Compose GPU access requires NVIDIA runtime setup and explicit GPU reservations. Source: [Docker Compose GPU docs](https://docs.docker.com/compose/how-tos/gpu-support/).\n\n## Recommended Architecture\nKeep the REST/API contract unchanged, but isolate inference behind a tiny backend interface and factory.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    cli[CLIArgs] --\u003e factory[DetectorFactory]\n    factory --\u003e tfliteBackend[TFLiteBackend]\n    factory --\u003e onnxBackend[OnnxBackend]\n    app[FastAPIApp] --\u003e detectorInterface[DetectorInterface]\n    detectorInterface --\u003e responseAdapter[PredictionsAdapter]\n    onnxBackend --\u003e ultralyticsRuntime[UltralyticsYOLO]\n    ultralyticsRuntime --\u003e ort[ONNXRuntime]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Proposed module split\n- Keep [&#96;prediction.py&#96;](prediction.py), [&#96;label.py&#96;](label.py), [&#96;confidence_evaluator.py&#96;](confidence_evaluator.py), and [&#96;prediction_saver.py&#96;](prediction_saver.py) as shared layers.\n- Extract a small backend contract, e.g. &#96;DetectorBackend.detect(np.ndarray) -\u003e Predictions&#96;.\n- Move the current TFLite implementation into a backend-specific module with no FastAPI knowledge.\n- Add an ONNX-specific backend module that:\n  - accepts &#96;.onnx&#96; models,\n  - delegates model loading/inference to Ultralytics where possible,\n  - converts Ultralytics detections into the existing &#96;Predictions&#96; schema,\n  - exposes explicit CPU/GPU selection and clear startup diagnostics.\n- Keep request parsing and response serialization in a backend-agnostic app/bootstrap layer.\n\n## Implementation Approach\n### 1. Validation-first spike\nBefore broad refactoring, validate the lowest-bespoke ONNX path:\n- confirm &#96;YOLO(\u003cmodel\u003e.onnx)&#96; works in a minimal service context,\n- confirm CPU and CUDA selection behave predictably,\n- inspect whether returned results provide boxes/classes/confidences that map directly into [&#96;Prediction&#96;](prediction.py),\n- confirm whether label-file loading is still needed or whether embedded model metadata can be preferred with label-file override.\n\nThis spike is important because you asked for &#96;broad ONNX compatibility&#96;. If Ultralytics already normalizes varied ONNX exports for us, that is materially better than implementing custom shape/postprocess branches by hand.\n\n### 2. Refactor around separation of concerns\nRefactor [&#96;main.py&#96;](main.py) into three concerns:\n- CLI/config parsing,\n- backend construction,\n- FastAPI app creation and lifecycle.\n\nThat enables both backends to share the same HTTP surface and save pipeline, and it makes future backends additive instead of invasive.\n\n### 3. Add explicit backend and device configuration\nIntroduce configuration that separates &#96;model family&#96; from &#96;execution target&#96;.\n\nRecommended shape:\n- &#96;--backend {tflite,onnx}&#96;\n- &#96;--model_file \u003cpath\u003e&#96;\n- &#96;--device cpu|cuda|cuda:0|cuda:1|...&#96;\n\nNotes:\n- Keep the existing TFLite device semantics for the current image if backward compatibility matters.\n- For the ONNX path, do not overload Coral/TPU values onto the same semantics; map ONNX values explicitly and fail fast on invalid combinations.\n- Preserve automatic backend inference from file suffix only as a convenience, not the primary control path.\n\n### 4. ONNX backend design\nPrimary path:\n- use Ultralytics as the model-facing layer for &#96;.onnx&#96; files,\n- keep our adapter layer limited to translating results into [&#96;Predictions&#96;](prediction.py),\n- keep shared save/filtering behavior unchanged.\n\nFallback policy:\n- if the validation spike shows Ultralytics cannot provide stable CPU/GPU selection or broad enough compatibility, fall back to a small ORT-native adapter for the standard Ultralytics detection ONNX shape only,\n- do not attempt a broad custom decoder matrix unless absolutely required, because that conflicts with your &#96;minimize bespoke code&#96; constraint.\n\n### 5. Docker and dependency strategy\nBecause you asked for a &#96;single fat image&#96;, the ONNX image should be treated as a separate artifact with its own dependency/runtime stack.\n\nRecommended packaging:\n- keep the current TFLite image path intact,\n- add a new ONNX-specific Dockerfile such as [&#96;Dockerfile.onnx&#96;](Dockerfile.onnx),\n- split dependencies into common vs backend-specific requirement sets so the ONNX image does not inherit EdgeTPU dependencies.\n\nRecommended dependency layout:\n- &#96;requirements-common.txt&#96;\n- &#96;requirements-tflite.txt&#96;\n- &#96;requirements-onnx.txt&#96;\n\nFor the ONNX image:\n- use a CUDA-capable base image consistent with the targeted &#96;onnxruntime-gpu&#96;/Ultralytics runtime expectations,\n- support CPU fallback inside the same image when GPU is not requested or not available,\n- emit clear startup logs about selected device/provider.\n\nCritical trade-off:\n- a single fat ONNX image is feasible, but it is not the minimal-size or minimal-complexity artifact. It increases image size and couples release/test strategy to CUDA compatibility.\n- This is still acceptable because it preserves a single operational image name while keeping the existing TFLite image separate.\n\n## Release And Integration Work\nUpdate [&#96;.github/workflows/publish.yml&#96;](.github/workflows/publish.yml) so release automation publishes both:\n- the existing image from [&#96;Dockerfile&#96;](Dockerfile), and\n- the new &#96;-onnx&#96; image from the ONNX Dockerfile.\n\nPrefer a build matrix or two explicit build steps rather than duplicating workflow logic.\n\n## Testing Plan\nAdd tests that protect the contract instead of backend internals:\n- API contract test for &#96;POST /detect&#96; multipart upload returning the exact &#96;Predictions&#96; shape.\n- Backend-selection tests for &#96;tflite&#96; vs &#96;onnx&#96; construction.\n- ONNX adapter tests using mocked Ultralytics results, so model binaries do not need to live in-repo.\n- Startup validation tests for invalid backend/device combinations.\n\nAlso fix the existing API inconsistency while touching the shared layer:\n- [&#96;main.py&#96;](main.py) currently returns &#96;{\"error\": \"Invalid image format\"}&#96; despite declaring &#96;response_model=Predictions&#96;.\n- Normalize this into proper HTTP error handling so both backends share correct behavior.\n\n## Documentation Updates\nUpdate [&#96;README.md&#96;](README.md) with:\n- ONNX export examples, including recommended Ultralytics export flags and supported scope,\n- CPU and GPU startup examples for the new image,\n- Docker Compose GPU example using NVIDIA device reservations,\n- explicit statement of what &#96;broad compatibility&#96; means in practice for v1 (for example: detection-focused &#96;.onnx&#96; models tested with Ultralytics/ORT, not arbitrary non-YOLO task graphs unless later validated).\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\nThe work is complete when:\n- the DeepStack-shaped REST contract remains unchanged for clients,\n- the current TFLite/Coral image still works unchanged,\n- a new &#96;-onnx&#96; image accepts &#96;.onnx&#96; models,\n- ONNX runtime selection supports &#96;cpu&#96; and &#96;cuda&#96; explicitly,\n- backend-specific code is isolated behind a narrow interface,\n- release automation publishes both artifacts,\n- tests cover contract stability and backend/device selection.\n",
```
